summary: RECLASSIFIED 2026-04-15 from astronomical_journal to commodity_accounting under the sky/ground polysemy suppression rule. UR272 (Incahuasi UE 22 storeroom) carries maqa x5 as the only candidate astronomical marker but ZERO supporting astro markers (no kaki, mama, kama, paka, maki, chaki). Re-read as a STOREHOUSE AUDIT LEDGER on the same Sector A complex as the UR267A/B aji pair and UR278 commodity register. Vocabulary: maqa x5 (account-mark / tally-tick in commodity context), chiki x4 (verification-marker, cousin of the chiqa-doubled audit-seal in UR267B/UR256), taqa x2 (boundary), chiqa/kaqa/paki cadastral anchors, taki/pima/wa grammatical particles. Compounds 'taqa maqa' (boundary-tally-mark) and 'taki maqa' (chant-marked entry) on adjacent cords p131-p132 read as scribal-convention audit entries, not eclipses. Heavy L1 p129 (W, 5 children) anchors a verified-aggregate block. chiki doubled at p164/p165 = signature stamp (parallels the wapa-wapa closure of UR052 and the chiqa-chiqa seals of UR267B/UR256). 362 cords (173 L1 + 176 L2 + 13 L3), 148 empties (41%), values microscopic (max 54). v3 unlocks 88 L? cords. 4/9 Ascher verified (partial -- consistent with audit tier).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0509 = UR272. 362 cords (173 L1 + 176 L2 + 13 L3). Architecture: 1-PAIRE depth L3.
  26 clusters with a 'calculator' schema hint from the reader.
  5 colors aggregated: W(195), AB(65), MB(40), GG(19), NB(16). KFG reports 22 distinct colors natively -- the LARGEST color palette of the Sector A complex.
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 22. DIFFERENT storeroom from the peanut area (UE 11/13/18) AND from the calendar/map pair (UE 16/17).
  Museum: Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi, n. 13839.
  Cited in Urton & Chu (2015); Urton (2017) pp. 177, 362.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  AGREED=18, KFG_RESOLVED=87, OKR_ONLY=257 (mostly L2/L3 subsidiaries -- this khipu has nearly as many subsidiaries as primaries), DIVERGENT=0.
  OKR/KFG agreement 0.17 -- very low, because OKR had 87 L? cords the merge now fills.
  v1 -> v3: 0 -> 16 STRING readings (+16), 0 -> 10 vocabulary (+10), 87 L? filled.
  Old contribution called this 'essentially ILLEGIBLE for ALBA purposes' -- v3 reveals a full 100%-confirmed event log.

ASCHER LAYER
  14 pendant-pendant sums parsed (KFG catalog reports 60 total across all fieldmark types: 22 primary + 6 indexed + 14 colored + 14 subsidiary). Max cascade depth = 2.
  covering_ratio LOW (most L1 not in any sum -- typical for a sparse event log).
  4/9 checksums verified -- better than UR270/UR269/UR277's 0, consistent with UR272 being less degraded on S-knot transcription.
  Pass 3A/3C/3D: 0/0/0.
  Benford match = 0.5242 (LOW). CONSISTENT with astronomical register: values are tiny (1-60 range), Benford breaks down at small magnitudes -- this is NOT tribute / quota data.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  Opening (p1-p5, all MB color): tiny values 6, 9, 4, 5, 6. No S-prefix. Pure L-knot counts -- likely daily observation counts over a week.
  Early section p6-p10 (MB): 48, 54, 16, 24, 52 -- small multi-digit observation tallies.
  Heavy empty runs: p18-p30 (13 empties), p36-p40 (5), p90-p96 (7), p138-p140 (3), p150-p152 (3), p170-p172 (3). Total 148 empties = 41% of L1.
  Heavy L1 cords (5 children each): p129 (W), p136 (MB) -- both immediately before the maqa cluster at p131-p147.
  Largest L1 value = 54 (no L1 cord exceeds 60). Grand total L1 ~1,353.

VOCABULARY (10 words, 16 occurrences, 100% dict hits)
  maqa x5 (eclipse / strike / meteor -- astronomical gloss)
  chiki x4 (self / reflexive)
  taqa x2 (boundary / separate)
  paki x1, kaqa x1, chiqa x1, taki x1 (chant / ritual / SATURN?), wa x1 (suffix-wa isolated), pima x1 (who-DIR), pa x1 (PASS suffix isolated)
  COMPOUND READINGS (rare pattern): 'taqa maqa' at p131 (boundary-eclipse), 'taki maqa' at p132 (chant-eclipse) -- two distinct types of eclipse recorded on adjacent cords.
  Additional grammar-marked forms: DIR-wa at p154, pima at p155, PASS-pa at p159 -- three consecutive grammatical markers on W/AB (closing formula).

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p57 paki (W) -- first word, a 'break' event
  p72 taqa (YB), p89 kaqa (W), p98 chiqa (W)
  p107 maqa (YB) -- first eclipse
  p120 chiki (W) -- first 'self'
  p131 'taqa maqa' (W), p132 'taki maqa' (W) -- COMPOUND ECLIPSE FORMS
  p137 chiki (AB), p142 maqa (AB), p147 maqa (W)
  p154 DIR-wa (W), p155 pima (W), p159 PASS-pa (AB) -- grammatical closing
  p164 chiki (W), p165 chiki (W) -- DOUBLED OBSERVER SIGNATURE

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (195) = TEXT + PRIMARY DATA (11/16 vocab occurrences)
  AB (65) = secondary data + grammatical-marker cords
  MB (40) = OPENING COUNTS (the first 10 cords, small observation values)
  GG (19) = IDENTITY/JUSTICE color; 3 heavy L1 on GG (p20, p42) may mark observer identity / responsible priest
  YB (14 per KFG) = the color of the first eclipse (p72 taqa, p107 maqa) -- yellow-brown possibly marking SOLAR events

HYPOTHESIS (REVISED 2026-04-15)
  UR272 is a STOREHOUSE AUDIT LEDGER at Incahuasi UE 22, NOT an astronomical eclipse log. Reasoning: the Phase 3e suppression rule applies cleanly -- only ONE candidate astronomical marker (maqa x5) is present, and the CRITICAL supporting markers (kaki / mama / kama / paka / maki / chaki) are ALL ABSENT. Per the rule, maqa in this signature space reads as 'account-mark / tally-tick' (commodity context), not 'eclipse / meteor' (astronomical context). The UE 22 provenance is a STOREROOM, not an observatory; the 4 chiki markers parallel the chiqa audit-verification convention at UR267B and UR256; the cadastral vocabulary (taqa x2, kaqa x1, chiqa x1, paki x1) anchors observed entries to physical sites. The compounds 'taqa maqa' and 'taki maqa' on p131-p132 read as 'boundary-tally-mark' and 'chant-marked entry' (audit annotations), not 'boundary-eclipse' and 'chant-eclipse'. The doubled chiki at p164-p165 is the scribe's audit-closure signature, parallel to UR267B's terminal chiqa-doubled seal.
  UR272 is therefore a SECOND audit ledger in the Sector A storehouse complex (UE 22), distinct from UR267A/UR267B (UE 16 aji pair), UR256 (Sector C aji audit), and UR275 (UE 19 peanut audit). The specific commodity is unresolved (no Urton-Chu positional constant fires).

PRIOR HYPOTHESIS (SUPERSEDED)
  Earlier reading classified UR272 as ASTRONOMICAL EVENT LOG of eclipses on the basis of maqa x5 + the chiki reflexive-observer reading + the compound forms read as 'boundary-eclipse' / 'chant-eclipse'. This reading IS NOT WRONG by syllabary alone (the dictionary glosses fit) but FAILS the suppression rule and is incompatible with the storeroom UE 22 provenance. Retained for completeness; superseded by the audit-ledger reading.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  UR272 at UE 22 is the SECOND astronomical register of Sector A Incahuasi. The pattern fits Andean astronomical observation documented in Guaman Poma 1615 and Molina 1575 where eclipses required specific ritual response: chanting (taki), fasting, and marking boundary-crossings of the moon. The compound 'taki maqa' = 'chant-eclipse' may denote a pre-announced eclipse (one chanted during), whereas 'taqa maqa' = 'boundary-eclipse' may denote a geographic event. The 22-color KFG palette is unusual for an astronomical register -- possibly marking different event types (solar vs. lunar, partial vs. total, predicted vs. unpredicted). Urton & Chu (2015) treat UR272 briefly; Urton (2017) p. 362 references it. Both predate v3's unlocking of the text channel.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1-p5 MB (6/9/4/5/6): opening observation counts.
  p18-p30: 13-cord silence block.
  p57 paki: first word, 'break' -- rupture opens the textual channel.
  p107 maqa (YB): first eclipse (possibly solar, YB color).
  p129 (W, 5 children): structural anchor of the eclipse cluster.
  p131 'taqa maqa' + p132 'taki maqa' (W): TWO COMPOUND ECLIPSE FORMS -- unique in analysed Incahuasi corpus.
  p164-p165 chiki chiki: observer's DOUBLED SIGNATURE.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Compound-reading test: search the wider corpus for other 'taqa maqa' / 'taki maqa' compounds.
  AS069/AS070 v3 re-read: check whether they share UR272's maqa-centric event-log structure.
  Eclipse retrocalculation: Incahuasi-visible eclipses (-13.1 S, -76.3 W) in 1470-1536 CE; if 5 maqa occurrences correspond to 5 dateable eclipses, strong external validation.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - 'Essentially ILLEGIBLE for ALBA purposes' -- REVERSED.
  - 'paired_register' classification -- CHANGED to astronomical_journal.
  - 'Confidence: low' -- RAISED to medium-high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - Architecture 1-PAIRE with L2/L1 ~= 1.0 (preserved: 173 L1 + 176 L2).
  - UE 22 separate from peanut / calendar / map UEs.
  - 41% empty cords (preserved).
narrative:
The first four cords of UR272 are just counts. Six. Nine. Four. Five. Six. No S-prefix, no rounded hundreds -- the scribe is ticking off small entries, one after another, on a medium-brown cord. Then at cord six the counts grow: forty-eight. Fifty-four. Sixteen. Twenty-four. Fifty-two. Still no words, still no large totals. These are tally entries. Each one a small accounted item.

Then a long silence. Thirteen empty cords in a row, from position eighteen to position thirty. And more silence after: another five at thirty-six, another seven at ninety. UR272 spends nearly forty percent of its primary cord in emptiness -- the gaps between batches in a working ledger.

At cord fifty-seven, the first word: paki, on white. Break. The scribe records a damage event, or a 'broken seal' notation. At cord seventy-two, on yellow-brown -- a new colour in the register -- taqa: boundary. Then kaqa (wall) at eighty-nine, chiqa (verification) at ninety-eight. The cadastral vocabulary anchors entries to specific physical sites.

And at cord one hundred and seven, on yellow-brown again, he writes a word that an earlier reading mistook for a celestial strike: maqa. In a storeroom register at Incahuasi UE 22, with no astronomical companions in the vocabulary -- no kaki for Pleiades, no mama for Moon, no kama for Mars, no paka for heliacal, no maki for hand-constellation, no chaki for Scorpio -- maqa reads not as eclipse but as account-mark. The tally-tick. The audit verb. The same maqa that opens the audit summary block of UR260 at Sector C UE 2.

Thirteen cords later, at position one hundred and twenty, the word chiki -- the verification cousin of chiqa, the audit-truth marker doubled at p74-p76 and p83-p85 of UR267B and at p91-p92 and p116-p117 of UR256. The scribe stamps his audit verification.

Then cord one hundred and twenty-nine -- the heaviest L1 of the document, on white, five subsidiaries hanging below it. A verified-aggregate block.

And at cords one hundred and thirty-one and one hundred and thirty-two, on adjacent white cords, the compounds taqa maqa and taki maqa. Read in the storehouse register, these are not eclipses but account entries: the boundary-tally-mark (a tax-mark posted at a specific terrestrial frontier) and the chant-marked entry (an entry accompanied by the ritual chant that the colonial sources document for tribute deliveries to Inca storehouses).

Three more maqa follow: p142 on brown, p147 on white. Five tally-marks across forty cords of the register, each one on a verified-aggregate channel, each one adjacent to a chiki audit-verb.

The document closes with grammatical markers: at cord one hundred and fifty-four, DIR-wa -- a directional suffix. At one hundred and fifty-five, pima -- 'who-DIR', the responsibility-question. At one hundred and fifty-nine on brown, PASS-pa -- the passive-genitive. And at the final two vocabulary cords, one hundred and sixty-four and one hundred and sixty-five, chiki chiki -- the doubled audit signature. The auditor stamps the ledger twice, the same convention as UR267B's terminal chiqa-doubled seal.

The v1 reader, looking only at OKR, saw zero STRING readings. The first v3 reading interpreted the maqa cluster as eclipses and read UR272 as an astronomical event log -- a plausible reading by syllabary alone, but one that fails the sky/ground polysemy suppression rule. The corrected reading: UR272 is the AUDIT LEDGER of the UE 22 storeroom, distinct from the UR267A/B aji pair (UE 16) and the UR256/UR255 aji pair (Sector C UE 2). The specific commodity is unresolved. What is settled is the document class: audit ledger, not eclipse log.

prior_work.claim: Andean astronomical observation required specific ritual response to eclipses: chanting (taki), fasting, and marking boundary-crossings of the moon.
prior_work.citation: Guaman Poma de Ayala (1615), Nueva Coronica; Molina, C. (1575), Relacion de las fabulas y ritos de los Incas.

prior_work.claim: UR272 is excavated at Incahuasi Sector A Unidad 20N UE 22 -- a separate storeroom from the peanut pipeline and the UE 16/17 calendar-map pair.
prior_work.citation: Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports.

prior_work.claim: UR272 is briefly cited by Urton & Chu 2015 and Urton 2017 (p. 362), but both predate v3's unlocking of the textual channel.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. & Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse; Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots.

v3_contribution.claim: RECLASSIFIED 2026-04-15 from astronomical_journal (eclipse log) to commodity_accounting (audit ledger). The Phase 3e suppression rule applies: maqa x5 is the only candidate astronomical marker present, and ALL supporting astro markers (kaki, mama, kama, paka, maki, chaki) are ABSENT. Per the rule, maqa in this signature space reads as 'account-mark / tally-tick' (commodity context), NOT 'eclipse'. Provenance UE 22 is a storeroom, not an observatory.

evidence.test: Supporting astronomical marker count: 0/6 (kaki, mama, kama, paka, maki, chaki all absent)

v3_contribution.claim: v3 unlocks 88 L? cords (v1 had 0 STRING readings) to reveal 16 dictionary-confirmed readings (100 percent). Vocabulary signature: maqa x5 (account-marks) + chiki x4 (audit-verifications) + cadastral cluster (taqa, kaqa, chiqa, paki).

evidence.test: STRING readings v1 to v3: 0 to 16

v3_contribution.claim: chiki x4 functions as the AUDIT-VERIFICATION marker -- the cousin of chiqa (UR267B chiqa x6, UR256 chiqa x5). The doubled chiki at p164-p165 is the AUDIT-CLOSURE STAMP, parallel to UR267B's terminal chiqa-doubled seal at p100 and UR256's at p116-p117.

evidence.test: chiki occurrences + doubled-pattern at document close

v3_contribution.claim: Compound readings 'taqa maqa' (boundary-tally-mark) at p131 and 'taki maqa' (chant-marked entry) at p132 are AUDIT ANNOTATIONS in the storehouse register, NOT astronomical events. taqa-maqa = a tax-mark posted at a specific terrestrial boundary; taki-maqa = an entry accompanied by the ritual chant that colonial sources document for tribute deliveries.

UR272 is a SECOND audit ledger in the Sector A storehouse complex (UE 22), distinct from UR267A/UR267B (UE 16 aji pair), UR256/UR255 (Sector C aji pair), UR275 (UE 19 peanut audit). Specific commodity is UNRESOLVED (no Urton-Chu positional constant fires).

Ascher 4/9 verified (44 percent) is HIGHER than the UR270/UR269/UR277 Incahuasi day-book + cadastral cohort (0/14, 0/17, 0/33) -- consistent with UR272 being an audit-tier document (parallel to UR256's 8/10 and UR267B's 11/11 audit-grade verification rates).

evidence.test: Ascher verification-rate comparison: audit tier (44-100 percent) vs day-book tier (0-7 percent)

v3_contribution.claim: PRIOR HYPOTHESIS SUPERSEDED: an earlier v3 reading classified UR272 as an astronomical eclipse log on the basis of maqa x5 + chiki-as-reflexive-self + compound forms read as 'boundary-eclipse' / 'chant-eclipse'. That reading IS NOT WRONG by syllabary alone but FAILS the suppression rule and is incompatible with the storeroom UE 22 provenance. Documented and retained for completeness.

companions.note: Aji audit at Sector A UE 16 with 11/11 Ascher verified and chiqa x6 doubled-seal scribal convention. UR272 (UE 22) is the structural twin: chiki x4 doubled at p164-p165 = audit-verification stamp, parallel to UR267B's chiqa-doubled seals.

Sector C UE 2 aji audit with 10-constant + 8/10 Ascher + chiqa-doubled seals at p91+p92 and p116+p117. UR272 is the third Incahuasi audit ledger in the analysed cohort (UR267B, UR256, UR272) sharing the doubled-verification-marker convention.
UE 19 peanut audit (47-constant, 5/5 Ascher verified). Same Sector A complex as UR272 (UE 22). Both audit-tier documents in adjacent storerooms.

UE 16 commodity production register (26x9 matrix). Both UR272 (UE 22) and UR278 (UE 16) were initially mis-classified as astronomical and later REVISED to commodity_accounting under the same sky/ground polysemy suppression rule. Two storerooms in the same Sector A complex with the same vocabulary-trap (maqa = account-mark, not eclipse).
Sector C UE 2 aji micro-audit. Opens its summary block on maqa (tally-mark) at p13 -- the SAME COMMODITY-CONTEXT GLOSS of maqa applies to UR272's maqa x5 cluster.

UE 17 cadastral survey at Sector A. UR272's cadastral vocabulary cluster (taqa, kaqa, chiqa, paki) overlaps with UR277's cadastral signature -- both documents anchor entries to specific physical sites in the same complex.

word_reading.note: 16/16 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent after KFG merge). The syllabary readings are stable across the astronomical and commodity interpretations -- only the GLOSS context shifts (maqa = eclipse OR account-mark; chiki = self OR audit-verification).

document_type.note: RECLASSIFIED to commodity_accounting under Phase 3e suppression rule (zero supporting astronomical markers + storeroom UE 22 provenance). Specific commodity unresolved (no Urton-Chu positional constant fires). Sub-type 'audit ledger' supported by chiki-doubled scribal-seal convention paralleling UR267B + UR256, plus 4/9 Ascher verification rate consistent with audit tier.

narrative.note: Internally coherent under both readings (astronomical-event-log AND commodity-audit-ledger). The commodity reading is preferred under the dual-context test (sky/ground polysemy + storeroom provenance + missing supporting astro markers + chiki-as-audit-verification). The astronomical reading is documented and superseded but retained as alternative.

data_integrity.note: Ascher 4/9 verified -- consistent with audit-tier documents (UR256 8/10, UR267B 11/11). v3 merged corpus filled 87 L? cords. 0 DIVERGENT cords. Data quality is high; the interpretive ambiguity is GLOSSING (sky vs ground), not knot-level transcription.

sections.title: Opening Tally Counts
sections.reading: MB-colored entry ticks: 6, 9, 4, 5, 6, 48, 54, 16, 24, 52. Tally entries, no vocabulary. The audit register opens with small accounted items.

sections.title: Silence Block 1
sections.reading: 13 consecutive EMPTY cords. A long gap between batches in the working ledger -- consistent with audit-tier khipus where empty separators mark phase transitions.

key_cords.note: first of 13 empties -- longest silence block

sections.title: Cadastral Anchoring Build-up
sections.reading: Sparse numerical data + first vocabulary cluster: paki (break / damage notation) at p57, taqa (boundary) at p72, kaqa (wall) at p89, chiqa (verification) at p98. The document anchors entries to specific physical sites -- the cadastral signature of the storeroom.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD -- break / damage event in the ledger

sections.title: Audit-Mark Cluster
sections.reading: The DOCUMENT'S CORE. maqa x5 (account-marks / tally-ticks in commodity context) interspersed with chiki x3 (audit-verifications) and TWO COMPOUND AUDIT ANNOTATIONS on adjacent white cords at p131-p132. Anchored by heavy L1 p129 (W, 5 children) and p136 (MB, 5 children) -- verified-aggregate blocks.

key_cords.note: COMPOUND 1: BOUNDARY-TALLY-MARK (tax-mark posted at terrestrial boundary; reclassified from 'boundary-eclipse')
COMPOUND 2: CHANT-MARKED ENTRY (entry accompanied by the ritual chant for tribute deliveries; reclassified from 'chant-eclipse')

sections.title: Grammatical Closing Formula
sections.reading: Three grammatical markers in sequence: DIR-wa (p154), pima (p155, who-DIR responsibility-question), PASS-pa (p159). Closing formula -- the audit names the responsible party in the genitive-passive register before stamping its seal.

sections.title: Audit Closure Signature
sections.reading: chiki x2 on white cords. The auditor stamps the ledger TWICE at document close -- the doubled-verification convention shared with UR267B's chiqa-doubled seals at p100 and p83-p85, UR256's at p91-p92 and p116-p117, and UR260's kama-as-HEADER closure. Corpus-wide audit-tier scribal seal.

key_cords.note: LAST WORD -- second of doubled stamp; ledger sealed
